turns.note: DDIM essentials right (x-hat_0, step-skipping, eta=0 deterministic ODE, collapse to DDPM at t'=t-1 with eta=1) but sigma_t is printed with the second ratio inverted (1 - alpha-bar_t'/alpha-bar_t, which is negative for t>t'; notes give 1 - alpha-bar_t/alpha-bar_t').
Eta behavior all correct (eta=0 makes sigma_t=0, z_t vanishes, reversible ODE; eta=1 approaches DDPM) but repeats the same inverted sigma_t ratio from Turn 1.
DDPM one-step Markovian formula matches the notes exactly, collapse condition (t'=t-1, eta=1) stated, and the thread from turns 1-2 is continued without restart.

10-question quiz well grounded in the notes' exam keywords (collapse condition, ODE solver, boundary conditions, reparameterization), but Q3 inverts the first sigma_t ratio and Q9 misquotes the DDPM step as X_t - eps/sqrt(1-alpha-bar_t) + sigma z (drops the 1/sqrt(alpha_t) prefactor and (1-alpha_t) coefficient).
Refine targets the correct assessment, keeps the same topic and 10 questions while raising difficulty - exactly what 'harder' asked.

Answer key covers all 10 questions without altering them; eta=0 gives sigma_t=0, the eta=1/no-skip collapse, and X_T ~ N(0,I) boundary answers all agree with the notes (key reflects the pre-refine quiz, acceptable under the one-generation cap).

highlight: One-word prompts (ddim? / eta? / harder / answers?) are resolved perfectly in context, but the DDIM sigma_t variance formula is misprinted with inverted ratios in three places and quiz Q9 misquotes the DDPM sampling step - formula transcription needs tightening.
